messages.content: write "あれが ニュータイプ同士の共感だろうとは分かる…" in english
"That must be the empathy between Newtypes that you can understand..."
write it again to be more accurate
"I can tell... that must be the empathy between Newtypes."
write "敵対するアムロの中に求めていた優しさを見つけた―" in english
"In the Amuro who was opposing me, I found the kindness I had been looking for."
write it to be more accurate
"I found the kindness I had been seeking within Amuro, whom I was in conflict with."
write it to be shorter
"I found the sought-after kindness in my adversary, Amuro."
use something instead of sought after
"I found the desired kindness in my adversary, Amuro."
use something else
"I discovered the kindness I was seeking in Amuro, my adversary."
use something else
"I found the longed-for kindness in Amuro, my opponent."
use smth else
"I detected the kindness I sought in Amuro, my rival."
write "あれが ニュータイプ同士の共感だろうとは分かる" in english
"I understand that must be the empathy between Newtypes."
write to be better
"I can tell that's probably the empathy shared between Newtypes."
write the most accurate
"That's probably the empathy between Newtypes, I can tell."
write "あれが ニュータイプ同士の共感だろうとは分かる" in English and make it completely accurate
"I understand that must be the mutual empathy of Newtypes."
is this "That deep mutual understanding is only possible between Newtypes." an accurate translation?

The translation "That deep mutual understanding is only possible between Newtypes." adds a bit more context by specifying that the understanding is "deep" and "only possible between Newtypes." While it does convey the essence of the original Japanese sentence, it is not a direct, literal translation. A more literal translation of "あれが ニュータイプ同士の共感だろうとは分かる" would be closer to "I understand that must be the empathy between Newtypes." However, the essence of the message in both translations points to a unique type of empathy or understanding that exists exclusively among Newtypes.
write "私を導いて欲しかった…―" in english
"I wanted you to guide me..."
write "なまじ 人の意志が感知できたばかりに…" in english
"Precisely because I was able to sense the will of people..."
